evals.prompt: I have a Django REST API project with postgres. I want to validate that the migrations are applied and the /api/posts/ endpoint returns real data, not a mock. What do I check? I'm concerned the DB might have migration errors.
evals.expected_output: Guide user through: (1) python manage.py check, (2) python manage.py showmigrations to spot [ ] unapplied, (3) python manage.py migrate to apply, (4) curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/posts/ to verify non-empty response. Emphasize checking the actual postgres, not in-memory test DB.

evals.prompt: My Flask app with Gunicorn is running on port 5000, but POST /api/users returns 403 Forbidden even though I'm admin. The app works locally. What's broken?
evals.expected_output: Point to CSRF token requirement (line 316 in Common Failures). Show: (1) curl -s -H 'X-CSRFToken: <token>' (if CSRF is enabled), or (2) check if the POST handler requires a specific header (Authorization, Content-Type). Validate by inspecting actual 403 response body with curl -i, not just the status.
